# cacert

Mozilla's CA certificate bundle, extracted into PEM form by the curl
project.  Compiled into `libice.a` as a byte array and supplied to
libcurl via `CURLOPT_CAINFO_BLOB` so HTTPS downloads work even when
the host system's CA trust store is missing or out of date — a common
failure mode on older or stripped-down Linux distributions.

The bundle ships *with* the binary; it is not fetched at runtime.  We
ignore the host's CA store entirely so the same `ice` binary verifies
TLS identically on every machine.

## Upstream

- Source:  https://curl.se/ca/cacert.pem
- Date:    2026-04-24
- License: MPL 2.0 (see [LICENSE](LICENSE))

The header at the top of `cacert.pem` records Mozilla's last-update
date and the SHA-256 of the bundle as published by curl.se; both serve
as the pinning record.

## Files

```
cacert.pem    Mozilla root CA bundle in PEM form, as published by curl.se.
ca_bundle.c   Generated byte array (`ca_bundle_pem` + `ca_bundle_pem_len`)
              compiled into libice.a and consumed by ca_certs.c at the
              project root.
Makefile      Re-sync recipe (no build step; ca_bundle.c is committed).
```

## Local patches

None.  Both files are reproduced verbatim from upstream / generated
from upstream by the recipe in [Makefile](Makefile).

## Re-sync

Refresh the bundled CA store from upstream:

```
make -C vendor/cacert refresh
```

This re-fetches `cacert.pem` from `https://curl.se/ca/cacert.pem` and
regenerates `ca_bundle.c`.  Update the **Date** in the section above
and commit both files.

Cadence: refresh at every `ice` release.  Mozilla updates the NSS root
store every two to three months (aligned with Firefox ESR cycles), and
curl.se republishes within days.  Out-of-cycle refreshes are warranted
when a widely-used CA is distrusted by Mozilla (e.g. the 2024 Entrust
distrust) or when a user reports a verifiable cert failure on a chain
that does verify against an up-to-date system store.
